name: Money Mart (previously The Check Cashing Store)
description_short: Money Mart (The Check Cashing Store) offers check cashing, cash advance loans, money transfers, and money orders at retail locations across multiple states.
description_long:
Money Mart, operating under the brand The Check Cashing Store, is a financial services company owned by Dollar Financial Group, Inc. The company has established a network of retail locations providing alternative financial services to consumers who may lack traditional banking access or need quick cash solutions.

The company's core service offerings include check cashing, cash advance loans, MoneyGram money transfers, money orders, mobile check cashing, small business services, and prepaid cards (Momentum Prepaid Mastercard). Cash advance loans are available in retail stores across Alaska, Arizona, California, Louisiana, and Florida, with online applications limited to California and Louisiana. Money orders, MoneyGram transfers, and check cashing services are available at their store locations.

Money Mart distinguishes itself through its multi-service approach, positioning itself as a "one-stop money services destination" rather than focusing on a single product. The company operates extended hours (9 AM-8 PM weekdays, 9 AM-6 PM Saturday) and provides a mobile app for check cashing functionality. Their prepaid card offering is issued by The Bancorp Bank and distributed through InComm Financial Services.

As a check-cashing and short-term lending provider, Money Mart serves consumers in financial transition but carries inherent limitations. The company explicitly disclaims that short-term loans should not be used as long-term financial solutions, and fees vary by state. Eligibility for loans depends on income verification and credit approval. Consumers should be aware that short-term lending can become expensive if used repeatedly.
pros: Extended store hours (9 AM-8 PM weekdays, 9 AM-6 PM Saturday) for convenience
Multiple services available at single locations: check cashing, loans, money transfers, money orders
Mobile check cashing available through their app
Cash advance loans available in-store in five states with flexible terms
MoneyGram money transfer services available both online and in-store
Prepaid Mastercard option (Momentum) for unbanked/underbanked consumers
Nationwide network with multiple store locations
cons: Check cashing fees vary by state and include per-item charges (specific rates not disclosed on website)
Cash advance loans only available in-store in 5 states (Alaska, Arizona, California, Louisiana, Florida); online applications limited to California and Louisiana
Company explicitly warns short-term loans can be expensive if used over months rather than weeks
Currency exchange fees apply to MoneyGram transfers
Standard financial services company disclaimers suggest limited transparency on full fee schedules
best_for: Consumers without bank accounts who need check cashing services
People in eligible states (AK, AZ, CA, LA, FL) seeking quick cash advances with store-based approval
Individuals needing money transfer services or money orders
Unbanked/underbanked consumers interested in a prepaid card option
diagnosis: Money Mart is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate check cashing, money transfers, or short-term loans and prefer in-person service with extended hours. The primary caveat is that cash advance loans are only available in five states and should be used for short-term financial needs only—the company acknowledges these products can become expensive if used repeatedly as a long-term solution.
services: Cash advance loans (in-store in AK, AZ, CA, LA, FL; online in CA, LA)
